name: ExtensionNutritionIntake_Occurrence
title: R5: The date/time or interval when the food or fluid is/was consumed (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `NutritionIntake.occurrence[x]` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`NutritionIntake.occurrence[x]` 0..1 `dateTime, Period`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `NutritionIntake.occurrence[x]` 0..1 `dateTime, Period`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `NutritionIntake.occurrence[x]` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionIntake` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionIntake.occurrence[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The date/time or interval when the food or fluid is/was consumed (new)
element.comment:
Element `NutritionIntake.occurrence[x]` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `NutritionIntake` is not mapped.
Element `NutritionIntake.occurrence[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This attribute reflects the period over which the patient consumed the food fluid and is expected to be populated on the majority of NutritionIntake. If the food or fluid is still being taken and is expected to continue indefinitely at the time the usage is recorded, the "end" date will be omitted.  If the end date is known, then it is included as the "end date".  The date/time attribute supports a variety of dates - year, year/month and exact date.  If something more than this is required, this should be conveyed as text.

element.short: The date/time or interval when the food or fluid is/was consumed
element.definition: The interval of time during which it is being asserted that the patient is/was consuming the food or fluid.
element.comment: This attribute reflects the period over which the patient consumed the food fluid and is expected to be populated on the majority of NutritionIntake. If the food or fluid is still being taken and is expected to continue indefinitely at the time the usage is recorded, the "end" date will be omitted.  If the end date is known, then it is included as the "end date".  The date/time attribute supports a variety of dates - year, year/month and exact date.  If something more than this is required, this should be conveyed as text.
